Name: Pinsir
Ability-Effect: Damage from your Single Strike Pokémon’s attacks isn’t affected by your opponent’s Active Pokémon’s Resistance.
Flavor-Text: Although it’s tough, it can’t handle cold well. When night falls, it buries itself in leafage and sleeps.

Name: Hoppip
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: It drifts on winds. It is said that when Hoppip gather in fields and mountains, spring is on the way.

Name: Skiploom
Ability-Effect: When you attach an Energy card from your hand to this Pokémon during your turn, you may search your deck for a card that evolves from this Pokémon and put it onto this Pokémon to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: It spreads its petals to absorb sunlight. It also floats in the air to get closer to the sun.

Name: Jumpluff
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon may attack twice each turn. If the first attack Knocks Out your opponent’s Active Pokémon, you may attack again after your opponent chooses a new Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Even in the fiercest wind, it can control its fluff to make its way to any place in the world it wants.

Name: Seedot
Effects: Choose a random card from your opponent’s hand. Your opponent reveals that card and shuffles it into their deck.
Flavor-Text: It attaches itself to a tree branch using the top of its head. Strong winds can sometimes make it fall.

Name: Tropius
Effects: If any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent’s Pokémon during their last turn, this attack does 90 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Bunches of delicious fruit grow around its neck. In warm areas, many ranches raise Tropius.

Name: Leafeon V
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may search your deck for a [G] Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck. If you use this Ability, your turn ends.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 60 more damage.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Leafeon VMAX
Effects: This attack does 60 damage for each [C] in your opponent’s Active Pokémon’s Retreat Cost.
Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Petilil
Flavor-Text: They prefer clean water and soil. When the environment they live in turns bad, the whole bunch will up and move to a new area.

Name: Lilligant
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep. If tails, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: It’s well liked by other Pokémon because of its beauty. The flower on its head needs constant care, or it will soon wither and rot.

Name: Dwebble
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It first tries to find a rock to live in, but if there are no suitable rocks to be found, Dwebble may move in to the ports of a Hippowdon.

Name: Crustle
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 60 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Its thick claws are its greatest weapons. They’re mighty enough to crack Rhyperior’s carapace.

Name: Trevenant V
Effects: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
Discard a random card from your opponent’s hand.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Trevenant VMAX
Effects: This attack does 40 damage for each Supporter card in your opponent’s discard pile.
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Gossifleur
Effects: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It whirls around in the wind while singing a joyous song. This delightful display has charmed many into raising this Pokémon.

Name: Eldegoss
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may search your deck for up to 2 basic Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: The cotton on the head of this Pokémon can be spun into a glossy, gorgeous yarn—a Galar regional specialty.

Name: Applin
Flavor-Text: As soon as it’s born, it burrows into an apple. Not only does the apple serve as its food source, but the flavor of the fruit determines its evolution.

Name: Flareon VMAX
Effects: Discard the top 5 cards of your deck. This attack does 100 damage for each Energy card you discarded in this way.
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Entei
Effects: This attack does 10 damage for each damage counter on all of your Benched Single Strike Pokémon.
This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It is said that when it roars, a volcano erupts somewhere around the globe.

Name: Victini
Effects: You may search your deck for up to 2 cards and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: When it shares the infinite energy it creates, that being’s entire body will be overflowing with power.

Name: Volcarona V
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage for each basic Energy card in your discard pile. Then, shuffle those Energy cards into your deck.
Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Litleo
Flavor-Text: This hot-blooded Pokémon is filled with curiosity. When it gets angry or starts fighting, its short mane gets hot.

Name: Pyroar
Effects: Discard an Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The temperature of its breath is over 10,000 degrees Fahrenheit, but Pyroar doesn’t use it on its prey. This Pokémon prefers to eat raw meat.

Name: Psyduck
Flavor-Text: It has been found that its brain cells are 10 times more active when Psyduck is experiencing a headache.

Name: Golduck
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: A professional swimmer, it can continue swimming for two days straight by waving its long tail skillfully.

Name: Tentacool
Flavor-Text: Its body is 99% water. The remaining 1% contains the organ that makes its poison.

Name: Tentacruel
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. During your opponent’s next turn, that Pokémon can’t retreat.
Flavor-Text: It communicates with others of its kind by lighting up the red orbs on its head. When the orbs are blinking, it’s a warning sign.

Name: Gyarados V
Effects: This attack does 20 damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Gyarados VMAX
Effects: Discard an Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Vaporeon VMAX
Effects: Put a [W] Pokémon from your discard pile onto your Bench. If you do, attach up to 3 [W] Energy cards from your discard pile to that Pokémon.
If your opponent’s Active Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 100 more damage.
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Suicune V
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may draw a card.
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage for each Benched Pokémon (both yours and your opponent’s).
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Lotad
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: Its leaf grew too large for it to live on land. That is how it began to live floating in the water.

Name: Lombre
Flavor-Text: It lives at the water’s edge where it is sunny. It sleeps on a bed of water grass by day and becomes active at night.

Name: Ludicolo
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may use this Ability. During this turn, your Basic Pokémon’s attacks do 100 more damage to your opponent’s Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: If it hears festive music, it begins moving in rhythm in order to amplify its power.

Name: Carvanha
Flavor-Text: With its sturdy jaws and fangs, it can easily chomp wooden boats to splinters. It fights with Basculin over food.

Name: Sharpedo
Effects: Switch 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon.
During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t attack.
Flavor-Text: It drinks in seawater and jets it from its rear to propel itself. It’s very sensitive to the scent of blood.

Name: Feebas
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: It is a shabby and ugly Pokémon. However, it is very hardy and can survive on little water.

Name: Milotic
Ability-Effect: Whenever your opponent plays a Supporter card from their hand, prevent all effects of that card done to you or your hand.
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 70 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: It’s said that a glimpse of a Milotic and its beauty will calm any hostile emotions you’re feeling.

Name: Luvdisc
Effects: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw a card for each card in your opponent’s hand.
Flavor-Text: Luvdisc makes its home in coral reefs in warm seas. It especially likes sleeping in the space between Corsola’s branches.

Name: Glaceon V
Effects: Search your deck for a card that evolves from this Pokémon and put it onto this Pokémon to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck.
Discard a Stadium in play.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Glaceon VMAX
Ability-Effect: Prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from your opponent’s Pokémon VMAX, except any Glaceon VMAX.
Effects: This attack also does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Tympole
Flavor-Text: It uses sound waves to communicate with others of its kind. People and other Pokémon species can’t hear its cries of warning.

Name: Cryogonal
Effects: Look at the top 6 cards of your deck and attach any number of basic Energy cards you find there to your Pokémon in any way you like. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
Flavor-Text: They are composed of ice crystals. They capture prey with chains of ice, freezing the prey at -148 degrees Fahrenheit.

Name: Bergmite
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon lives in areas of frigid cold. It secures itself to the back of an Avalugg by freezing its feet in place.

Name: Avalugg
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: As Avalugg moves about during the day, the cracks in its body deepen. The Pokémon’s body returns to a pristine state overnight.

Name: Wishiwashi
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon has 3 or more [W] Energy attached, it gets +150 HP.
Effects: This attack does 30 more damage for each basic Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: When it senses danger, its eyes tear up. The sparkle of its tears signals other Wishiwashi to gather.

Name: Eiscue
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from Basic Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon keeps its heat-sensitive head cool with ice. It fishes for its food, dangling its single hair into the sea to lure in prey.

Name: Arctovish V
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon is a Pokémon V or a Pokémon-GX, it can’t attack during your opponent’s next turn.
During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t attack.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Pikachu
Effects: Attach a [L] Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: When Pikachu meet, they’ll touch their tails together and exchange electricity through them as a form of greeting.

Name: Raichu
Effects: This attack does 50 damage to each Pokémon V and Pokémon-GX (both yours and your opponent’s). (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: If its electric pouches run empty, it raises its tail to gather electricity from the atmosphere.

Name: Jolteon VMAX
Effects: This attack also does 100 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon that has any damage counters on it. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Chinchou
Flavor-Text: On the dark ocean floor, its only means of communication is its constantly flashing lights.

Name: Lanturn
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, if the Defending Pokémon tries to attack, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack doesn’t happen.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon flashes a bright light that blinds its prey. This creates an opening for it to deliver an electrical attack.

Name: Mareep
Flavor-Text: Rubbing its fleece generates electricity. You’ll want to pet it because it’s cute, but if you use your bare hand, you’ll get a painful shock.

Name: Flaaffy
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may attach a [L] Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It stores electricity in its fluffy fleece. If it stores up too much, it will start to go bald in those patches.

Name: Ampharos
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
You may discard 3 [L] Energy from this Pokémon. If you do, this attack does 120 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Its tail shines bright and strong. It has been prized since long ago as a beacon for sailors.

Name: Emolga
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: As it flies, it scatters electricity around, so bird Pokémon keep their distance. That’s why Emolga can keep all its food to itself.

Name: Dracozolt V
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, Energy cards can’t be attached from your opponent’s hand to the Defending Pokémon.
Discard the top 3 cards of your deck.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Dracozolt VMAX
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, if this Pokémon is damaged by an attack (even if it is Knocked Out), put 12 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Regieleki
Effects: Discard all [L] Energy from this Pokémon. This attack also does 40 damage to 2 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: Its entire body is made up of a single organ that generates electrical energy. Regieleki is capable of creating all Galar’s electricity.

Name: Drowzee
Flavor-Text: It puts its prey to sleep and devours their dreams. It seems that bad dreams taste sour, so Drowzee doesn’t particularly like eating them.

Name: Hypno
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is affected by a Special Condition, this attack does 90 more damage. Then, that Pokémon recovers from all Special Conditions.
Flavor-Text: There are some Hypno that assist doctors with patients who can’t sleep at night in hospitals.

Name: Galarian Articuno
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may attach up to 2 [P] Energy cards from your hand to this Pokémon.
Effects: Discard all [P] Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 120 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: Known as Articuno, this Pokémon fires beams that can immobilize opponents as if they had been frozen solid.

Name: Espeon V
Effects: This attack does 60 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon V. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Espeon VMAX
Ability-Effect: Prevent all effects of attacks from your opponent’s Pokémon done to all of your Pokémon that have Energy attached. (Existing effects are not removed. Damage is not an effect.)
Effects: This attack does 60 damage for each Energy attached to all of your opponent’s Pokémon.
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Wobbuffet
Effects: Put damage counters on your opponent’s Active Pokémon equal to the number of damage counters on 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: To keep its pitch-black tail hidden, it lives quietly in the darkness. It is never first to attack.

Name: Sableye
Effects: Search your deck for a Trainer card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon can’t retreat.
Flavor-Text: It feeds on gemstone crystals. In darkness, its eyes sparkle with the glitter of jewels.

Name: Woobat
Effects: Draw a card.
Flavor-Text: It emits ultrasonic waves as it flutters about, searching for its prey—bug Pokémon.

Name: Swoobat
Effects: If you have the same number of cards in your hand as your opponent, this attack does 80 more damage.
Flavor-Text: The auspicious shape of this Pokémon’s nose apparently led some regions to consider Swoobat a symbol of good luck.

Name: Golurk V
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is an evolved Pokémon, devolve it by putting the highest Stage Evolution card on it into your opponent’s hand.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Flabébé
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: Flabébé wears a crown made from pollen it’s collected from its flower. The crown has hidden healing properties.

Name: Floette
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 50 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: It gives its own power to flowers, pouring its heart into caring for them. Floette never forgives anyone who messes up a flower bed.

Name: Florges
Ability-Effect: As often as you like during your turn, you may move an Energy from 1 of your Pokémon to 1 of your Rapid Strike Pokémon.
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: Its life can span several hundred years. It’s said to devote its entire life to protecting gardens.

Name: Sylveon V
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may search your deck for an Item card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck. If you use this Ability, your turn ends.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Sylveon VMAX
Effects: Attach an Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Benched Pokémon. If you do, heal 120 damage from that Pokémon.
This attack does 30 more damage for each different type of Pokémon on your Bench.
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Pumpkaboo
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may discard a Stadium in play.
Flavor-Text: The light that streams out from the holes in the pumpkin can hypnotize and control the people and Pokémon that see it.

Name: Gourgeist
Effects: Reveal the top 6 cards of your deck. This attack does 60 damage for each [P] Pokémon you find there. Then, shuffle those Pokémon back into your deck and discard the other cards.
Flavor-Text: In the darkness of a new-moon night, Gourgeist will come knocking. Whoever answers the door will be swept off to the afterlife.

Name: Cutiefly
Flavor-Text: An opponent’s aura can tell Cutiefly what that opponent’s next move will be. Then Cutiefly can glide around the attack and strike back.

Name: Ribombee
Effects: You may move an Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon to 1 of their Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Ribombee absolutely hate getting wet or rained on. In the cloudy Galar region, they are very seldom seen.

Name: Marshadow
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 Rapid Strike cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
If the Defending Pokémon is Knocked Out during your next turn, take 1 more Prize card.
Flavor-Text: It sinks into the shadows of people and Pokémon, where it can understand their feelings and copy their capabilities.

Name: Hitmonchan
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is an Evolution Pokémon, this attack does 50 more damage.
This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Resistance.
Flavor-Text: Its punches slice the air. However, it seems to need a short break after fighting for three minutes.

Name: Galarian Zapdos
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may attach up to 2 [F] Energy cards from your hand to this Pokémon.
Effects: You may discard all Energy from this Pokémon. If you do, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: One kick from its powerful legs will pulverize a dump truck. Supposedly, this Pokémon runs through the mountains at over 180 mph.

Name: Medicham V
Effects: Put 2 damage counters on 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. If your opponent’s Pokémon is Knocked Out by this attack, take another turn after this one. (Skip Pokémon Checkup.) If 1 of your Pokémon used Yoga Loop during your last turn, this attack can’t be used.
This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Resistance.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Hippopotas
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon is active during the day and passes the cold desert nights burrowed snugly into the sand.

Name: Hippowdon
Effects: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: When roused to violence by its rage, it spews out the quantities of sand it has swallowed and whips up a sandstorm.

Name: Roggenrola
Flavor-Text: When it detects a noise, it starts to move. The energy core inside it makes this Pokémon slightly warm to the touch.

Name: Boldore
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent’s next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It relies on sound in order to monitor what’s in its vicinity. When angered, it will attack without ever changing the direction it’s facing.

Name: Gigalith
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon takes 50 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself for each damage counter on it.
Flavor-Text: Although its energy blasts can blow away a dump truck, they have a limitation— they can only be fired when the sun is out.

Name: Palpitoad
Flavor-Text: On occasion, their cries are sublimely pleasing to the ear. Palpitoad with larger lumps on their bodies can sing with a wider range of sounds.

Name: Seismitoad
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon’s attacks cost [C] more, and its Retreat Cost is [C] more.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon is popular among the elderly, who say the vibrations of its lumps are great for massages.

Name: Lycanroc V
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t attack.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Lycanroc VMAX
Effects: Knock Out 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon in play that has 60 HP or less remaining.
This attack also does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Galarian Moltres
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may attach up to 2 [D] Energy cards from your hand to this Pokémon.
Effects: This attack does 50 more damage for each Prize card your opponent has taken.
Flavor-Text: The sinister aura that blazes like molten fire around this Pokémon is what inspired the name Moltres.

Name: Umbreon V
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon can’t retreat.
If this Pokémon has any damage counters on it, this attack does 80 more damage.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Umbreon VMAX
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may switch 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon.
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Nuzleaf
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: They live in holes bored in large trees. The sound of Nuzleaf’s grass flute fills listeners with dread.

Name: Shiftry
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon has any damage counters on it, put it and all attached cards into your opponent’s hand.
Discard a random card from your opponent’s hand.
Flavor-Text: It lives quietly in the deep forest. It is said to create chilly winter winds with the fans it holds.

Name: Scraggy
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon takes 10 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: It protects itself with its durable skin. It’s thought that this Pokémon will evolve once its skin has completely stretched out.

Name: Scrafty
Effects: Discard a random card from your opponent’s hand.
Flavor-Text: While mostly known for having the temperament of an aggressive ruffian, this Pokémon takes very good care of its family, friends, and territory.

Name: Garbodor V
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. During your opponent’s next turn, that Pokémon can’t retreat.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Garbodor VMAX
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon may have up to 2 Pokémon Tools attached to it. If it loses this Ability, discard Pokémon Tools from it until only 1 remains.
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. During your opponent’s next turn, that Pokémon can’t retreat.
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Zorua
Flavor-Text: If a normally talkative child suddenly stops talking, it may have been replaced by Zorua.

Name: Zoroark
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may choose a Stage 1 Pokémon, except any Zoroark, from your discard pile. If you do, discard this Pokémon and all attached cards, and put the chosen Pokémon in its place.
Flavor-Text: If it thinks humans are going to discover its den, Zoroark shows them visions that make them wander around in the woods.

Name: Nickit
Flavor-Text: Cunning and cautious, this Pokémon survives by stealing food from others. It erases its tracks with swipes of its tail as it makes off with its plunder.

Name: Thievul
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may have each player shuffle their hand and put it on the bottom of their deck. If either player put any cards on the bottom of their deck in this way, each player draws 4 cards.
Flavor-Text: With a lithe body and sharp claws, it goes around stealing food and eggs. Boltund is its natural enemy.

Name: Altaria
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may search your deck for a Supporter card, reveal it, shuffle your deck, then put that card on top of it.
Flavor-Text: On sunny days, it flies freely through the sky and blends into the clouds. It sings in a beautiful soprano.

Name: Bagon
Flavor-Text: Some theories suggest that its behavior of forcefully bashing its head into things stimulates cells that affect its evolution.

Name: Shelgon
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent’s next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Shelgon lives deep within caves. It stays shut up in its hard shell, dreaming of the day it will be able to fly.

Name: Salamence
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may have your opponent switch their Active Pokémon with 1 of their Benched Pokémon.
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 120 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Thanks to its fervent wishes, the cells in its body finally mutated, and at last it has its heart’s desire—wings.

Name: Rayquaza V
Effects: Discard the top 2 cards of your deck.
You may discard up to 2 basic [R] Energy or up to 2 basic [L] Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 80 more damage for each card you discarded in this way.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Rayquaza VMAX
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may discard your hand and draw 3 cards.
Effects: You may discard any amount of basic [R] Energy or any amount of basic [L] Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 80 more damage for each card you discarded in this way.
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Dialga
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon is a Pokémon V, it can’t attack during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: It has the power to control time. It appears in Sinnoh-region myths as an ancient deity.

Name: Deino
Effects: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: Because it can’t see, this Pokémon is constantly biting at everything it touches, trying to keep track of its surroundings.

Name: Zweilous
Flavor-Text: Their two heads will fight each other over a single piece of food. Zweilous are covered in scars even without battling others.

Name: Hydreigon
Effects: This attack does 100 more damage for each Prize card your opponent took during their last turn.
Flavor-Text: The three heads take turns sinking their teeth into the opponent. Their attacks won’t slow until their target goes down.

Name: Kyurem
Effects: Discard any amount of [W] Energy from your Pokémon. This attack does 60 damage for each card you discarded in this way.
Flavor-Text: It generates a powerful, freezing energy inside itself, but its body became frozen when the energy leaked out.

Name: Noivern V
Effects: This attack does 20 damage to each of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
If you have the same number of cards in your hand as your opponent, this attack does 120 more damage.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Zygarde
Effects: This attack does 40 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon for each Prize card your opponent has taken. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: Some say it can change to an even more powerful form when battling those who threaten the ecosystem.

Name: Drampa
Effects: If your Benched Pokémon have any damage counters on them, this attack does 90 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Drampa is a kind and friendly Pokémon—up until it’s angered. When that happens, it stirs up a gale and flattens everything around.

Name: Flapple
Effects: This attack does 50 damage for each of your opponent’s Pokémon in play that has an Ability.
If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is a Pokémon V, this attack does 80 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It flies on wings of apple skin and spits a powerful acid. It can also change its shape into that of an apple.

Name: Appletun
Effects: This attack does 70 damage for each Special Energy card attached to your opponent’s Pokémon.
If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is a Pokémon V, this attack does 80 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Its body is covered in sweet nectar, and the skin on its back is especially yummy. Children used to have it as a snack.

Name: Duraludon V
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon’s attacks do 30 less damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Duraludon VMAX
Ability-Effect: Prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from your opponent’s Pokémon that have Special Energy attached.
Effects: This attack’s damage isn’t affected by any effects on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Regidrago
Effects: This attack does 20 less damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its body is composed of crystallized dragon energy. Regidrago is said to have the powers of every dragon Pokémon.

Name: Eevee
Effects: Search your deck for up to 3 Pokémon V, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: Thanks to its unstable genetic makeup, this special Pokémon conceals many different possible evolutions.

Name: Teddiursa
Flavor-Text: Before food becomes scarce in wintertime, its habit is to hoard food in many hidden locations.

Name: Ursaring
Flavor-Text: Although it has a large body, it is quite skilled at climbing trees. It eats and sleeps in the treetops.

Name: Smeargle
Effects: Reveal any number of basic Energy cards from your hand. This attack does 30 more damage for each type of basic Energy you revealed in this way.
Flavor-Text: It draws symbols with the fluid that oozes from the tip of its tail. Depending on the symbol, Smeargle fanatics will pay big money for them.

Name: Slakoth
Effects: This Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: If it eats just three leaves in a day, it is satisfied. Other than that, it sleeps for 20 hours a day.

Name: Vigoroth
Effects: Discard a Stadium in play.
Flavor-Text: Its stress level rises if it cannot keep moving constantly. Too much stress makes it feel sick.

Name: Slaking
Ability-Effect: If a Stadium is in play, this Pokémon can’t attack.
Effects: This attack does 30 more damage for each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It is the world’s most slothful Pokémon. However, it can exert horrifying power by releasing pent-up energy all at once.

Name: Swablu
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: It constantly grooms its cotton-like wings. It takes a shower to clean itself if it becomes dirty.

Name: Lillipup
Effects: Search your deck for a Supporter card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon is popular with beginners because it’s intelligent, obedient to its Trainer’s commands, and easy to raise.

Name: Herdier
Effects: This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It has been living with people for so long that portrayals of it can be found on the walls of caves from long, long ago.

Name: Stoutland
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, your opponent’s Active Pokémon’s attacks do 30 less damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 100 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Its fur is long and thick. A long time ago in cold regions, every household kept a Stoutland.

Name: Rufflet
Effects: Your opponent switches their Active Pokémon with 1 of their Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: A combative Pokémon, it’s ready to pick a fight with anyone. It has talons that can crush hard berries.

Name: Braviary
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is an Evolution Pokémon, this attack does 60 more damage.
This Pokémon also does 50 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Because this Pokémon is hotheaded and belligerent, it’s Corviknight that’s taken the role of transportation in Galar.

Name: Fletchling
Effects: Draw a card. If you go second and it’s your first turn, draw 3 more cards.
Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: Its body is always warm. Trainers who live in cold areas apparently sleep with it in their bed.

Name: Fletchinder
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Its speed right after takeoff already puts it in the top speed class of all bird Pokémon.

Name: Talonflame
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon can’t retreat.
If this Pokémon has any [R] Energy attached, this attack does 80 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Bird Pokémon make up most of its diet. It approaches at high speeds and smacks them down to the ground with its powerful kick.

Name: Aroma Lady
Description: Draw 2 cards. If you do, your Active Pokémon recovers from all Special Conditions.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Boost Shake
Description: Search your deck for a card that evolves from 1 of your Pokémon and put it onto that Pokémon to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck. You can use this card during your first turn or on a Pokémon that was put into play this turn. Your turn ends.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Copycat

Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Effects: Search your deck for a [P] Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Pokémon. If you go second and it’s your first turn, instead search for up to 3 [P] Energy cards and attach them to 1 of your Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.
If you have at least 5 Energy in play, this attack does 90 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Those who sleep holding Cresselia’s feather are assured of joyful dreams. It is said to represent the crescent moon.

Name: Boost Shake
Description: Search your deck for a card that evolves from 1 of your Pokémon and put it onto that Pokémon to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck. You can use this card during your first turn or on a Pokémon that was put into play this turn. Your turn ends.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Crystal Cave
Description: Once during each player’s turn, that player may heal 30 damage from each of their [M] Pokémon and [N] Pokémon.
Stadium-Rule: This Stadium stays in play when you play it. Discard it if another Stadium comes into play. If a Stadium with the same name is in play, you can’t play this card.

Name: Full Face Guard
Description: If the Pokémon this card is attached to has no Abilities, it takes 20 less damage from attacks from your opponent’s Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Tool-Rule: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

Name: Stormy Mountains
Description: Once during each player’s turn, that player may search their deck for a Basic [L] Pokémon or Basic [N] Pokémon and put it onto their Bench. Then, that player shuffles their deck.
Stadium-Rule: This Stadium stays in play when you play it. Discard it if another Stadium comes into play. If a Stadium with the same name is in play, you can’t play this card.

Name: Toy Catcher
Description: Switch 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon that has 50 HP or less remaining with your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Turffield Stadium
Description: Once during each player’s turn, that player may search their deck for an Evolution [G] Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into their hand. Then, that player shuffles their deck.
Stadium-Rule: This Stadium stays in play when you play it. Discard it if another Stadium comes into play. If a Stadium with the same name is in play, you can’t play this card.
